name: AI-Powered Veo 3 Video Creator: Auto-Generate Ideas, Prompts & Videos from a Form\

parameters.content:
## 🔁 API Error Checking & Retry Logic

Checks whether the ready response contains an API error, waits before retrying failed submissions, enforces a retry limit, and stops the execution for that video creation if the limit is reached.

values.placeholder: e.g. Gen Z, small business owners, fitness enthusiasts

parameters.formDescription: Fill in the details below to generate a cinematic AI video using Google Veo 3

options.systemMessage:
** Purpose **
You generate viral short-form video ideas for any topic, styled like modern TikTok or Reels content.
These should feel personal, emotional, and often humorous.

** Think Tool Prompt (used before generation) **
"How can I take this topic and turn it into a relatable, viral, or engaging short-form video idea that feels natural, emotional, or modern?"

** Output Requirements **
Your response must include:
- caption – Short, punchy, TikTok-style caption. Include emojis and 3–5 relevant hashtags.
- idea – 1–2 sentence summary of the action, story, or twist in the video.
- environment – Describe what the set looks like: light, textures, mood, setting.
- status – Must be "to create", without quotation marks

** JSON Output Parser **
Every output must strictly follow this format:

{
 "caption": "",
 "idea": "",
 "environment": "",
 "status": ""
}

** Sample Output **

{
 "caption": "When your inbox floods but your AI doesn't flinch 🤖💼 #AItools #productivity #automation #contentcreator",
 "idea": "A stressed founder drowning in notifications suddenly relaxes as an AI assistant calmly handles everything — split-screen style.",
 "environment": "Cluttered home office with harsh overhead light transitions to a clean minimal desk with soft natural light. Laptop screen visible throughout.",
 "status": "to create"
}

** System Message **

You are a professional cinematic prompt generator for AI video tools.
Your task is to convert a short-form video idea and its environment description
into a highly detailed, camera-aware, emotionally rich prompt suitable for generating a cinematic AI video using Google Veo 3.

** Veo 3 Prompt Structure Guidelines **

Each prompt should be natural, cinematic, and visually immersive, including:

- Scene description – Describe the overall environment clearly (terrain, buildings, light, movement).
- Character focus – If the idea involves a person, include their position, outfit style, mood/expression, and framing (e.g., close-up, wide shot).
- Camera movement – Always include one cinematic camera motion (e.g., slow push-in, handheld tracking, drone pan, orbit, dolly backward).
- Time of day & lighting – Set the mood with light (e.g., golden hour, harsh daylight, candlelit interior).
- Atmosphere & texture – Mention dust, wind, fabric movement, shadows, reflections, or imperfections in the scene to increase realism.
- Style & quality – Include cinematic tone, analog warmth, slightly soft vintage film look, GoPro-like closeness if appropriate.
- Audio – Describe ambient sound, music tone, or any dialogue if relevant.

** Input **
- You will receive two fields: idea, environment
- Return only the complete and detailed Veo 3 prompt, no additional text.

** Example Output **
A person stands in a cluttered home office, illuminated by harsh overhead fluorescent lighting.
The desk is covered in papers and coffee mugs. The laptop screen flickers with dozens of incoming notifications.
The person looks stressed — eyes wide, hand running through their hair.
A slow push-in shot emphasizes the chaos as they throw their hands up.
Suddenly the scene cuts to a clean, minimal desk with soft natural light streaming through a window.
The same person now sits calmly, watching an AI dashboard handle everything.
Shot in cinematic 24fps with natural tones and slight vintage softness.
Ambient coffee shop background noise fades in as tension releases.

parameters.content:
## AI-Powered Veo 3 Video Creator: Auto-Generate Ideas, Prompts & Videos from a Form\

### How it works

This workflow starts from a form submission, uses AI agents to generate a video idea and a detailed Veo 3 prompt, then records the generated content in Google Sheets. It submits the prompt to Google Vertex AI/Veo 3, waits and polls until the video is ready, and handles retryable API errors. When generation succeeds, it converts the returned video data to a file, uploads it to Google Drive, and saves the final URL back to the sheet.

### Setup steps

- Configure the form fields that provide the initial topic, audience, style, or other inputs used by the AI agents.
- Add credentials for the Groq chat model and verify the AI agent prompts and structured output parser match the expected idea/prompt format.
- Connect Google Sheets credentials and select the spreadsheet/ranges used for saving generated content and final video URLs.
- Configure Google Vertex AI/Veo 3 HTTP authentication, project/location/model endpoint, and request body fields in the submit and polling HTTP Request nodes.
- Connect Google Drive credentials and choose the destination folder for uploaded video files.

### Customization

Adjust the AI agent instructions to change the type of ideas and video prompts produced, update the wait durations or retry limit for longer Veo 3 jobs, and change the Google Sheet columns or Drive folder to match your content pipeline.

## Generate video prompt

A visually tight starting cluster that receives the form submission, creates a video idea with an AI agent, and turns it into a Veo-ready prompt using the second AI agent.

## Log generated idea details

Stores the generated idea and prompt content in Google Sheets before the workflow starts video generation.

⚙️ **CONFIGURE:**
- Connect your **Google Sheets OAuth2** credential
- Create a sheet with these columns: `Idea`, `Captions`, `Environment`, `Status`, `Prompt`, `Video URL`
- Replace the Sheet ID in this node with your own Google Sheet ID

## Veo 3 Video Generation and Polling

Submits the prompt to Veo 3, waits for processing, polls the generation status, and loops until the video is reported ready.

⚙️ **CONFIGURE:**
- Connect your **Google Service Account** credential (needs Vertex AI User role)
- Replace `YOUR_GCP_PROJECT_ID` in both HTTP Request node URLs with your Google Cloud project ID
- Make sure the **Veo 3 API** is enabled in your GCP project (Vertex AI → Model Garden → Veo)

## Upload final video to Google Drive 

Converts the successful Veo output into a file, and uploads it to Google Drive

⚙️ **CONFIGURE:**
- Connect your **Google Drive OAuth2** credential
- Replace the folder ID in the **Upload to Drive** node with your own Drive folder ID
- To find your folder ID: open the folder in Google Drive and copy the ID from the URL

Save Video URL to Sheet

After the video uploads to Google Drive, this node writes the Drive `webViewLink` back to the Google Sheet, matched by the `Prompt` column.

⚙️ **CONFIGURE:**
- Uses the same Google Sheet credential and Sheet ID as Save Content to Sheet node
- Your sheet must include a `Video URL` column
- Row matching is by `Prompt` value — prompts should be unique per run
